skill: One-variable data: Distributions and measures of center and spread
stem: The weights, in kilograms, for 17 cattle on a farm were reported, and the mean, median, range, and standard deviation for the data were found. The cow with the highest reported weight was later found to actually weigh 15 kilograms more than its reported weight. Which value remains unchanged when the four statistics are recomputed using the corrected weight?
rationale: Choice C is correct. The corrected weight is still the maximum, so the relative ordering of values does not change. The middle value (median) is therefore unchanged. The mean changes because the total changes, the range changes because the maximum changes, and the standard deviation changes because deviations from the new mean differ.
